explicit_text: I was born in Dalat, Vietnam, and my early memories are tied to this place. When I was five, on my golden birthday, my grandpa took me to dinner there and gave me a lucky charm from Japan. Dalat is where my story begins and where my connection to my grandpa and my childhood was rooted.

explicit_text: Vietnam is my homeland where I grew up before moving to the United States. My family and I lived there, and it’s where my mom made me a lucky charm before I left. Leaving Vietnam was a big step, and that charm was meant to wish me luck as I started a new life abroad.

explicit_text: Japan plays an important role in my story because my grandpa gave me an omamori, a lucky charm from Japan, when I was a child. I also passed through a Japanese airport on my way to the United States, where I almost lost that charm. Japan is connected to my journey and the protective symbol my grandpa believed would keep me safe.

explicit_text: The omamori is a lucky charm from Japan that my grandpa gave me when I was five because I was often sick. It has been with me for over 15 years and has been a source of comfort through my highest and lowest moments. Losing it at a Japanese airport was terrifying, but thankfully a cleaning lady found it and returned it to me, which made me realize how important it is as a connection to my grandpa.

explicit_text: I came to the United States of America not knowing what to expect, so I packed everything I thought I might need, including clothes, ramen noodles, and books. My journey included a stop in Japan, and arriving in the USA marked a big change in my life. The lucky charm from my grandpa was especially important during this transition, reminding me to be myself and stay connected to my roots.
